claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - his name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Now I can help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me ask you the first question:

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 12, he's under 16, which is good for eligibility purposes.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now let me check a few more important details:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions to make sure I have the full picture:

**Is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or does he have any special living arrangements I should know about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. He's just living at home with me normally.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete the assessment:

**Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a UK citizen with the right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 12 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK and are a UK citizen
- Alex lives with you at home
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He's not in hospital or care

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive Child Benefit, but may need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

If you'd like to proceed with claiming, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. You are responsible for Alex as he lives with you at home in the UK. You are a UK citizen living in the UK, which satisfies the residence requirements. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 12-year-old son Alex. Alex lives at home with the claimant under normal circumstances, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and he is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
